title: elymus multinodus
intro: Elymus multinodus, also known as the many-noded wheatgrass or many-noded wildrye, is a type of perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of China, Nepal, and Tibet. Traditionally, the plant is used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, alkaloids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Elymus multinodus include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and protecting against cardiovascular diseases. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are often used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are also dried for use in capsules or as a powder for various health applications.
